message:
fix(output): re-enable error panel border_style in OutputContext::error

`OutputContext::error(message)` had its `.border_style(...)` line
commented out (with a `// border_style missing?` note) since the
prior refactor that gated the theme through a `theme()` accessor.
Result: Rich-mode error panels rendered with the default border
colour instead of the error tone, making them visually
indistinguishable from the surrounding info-tone output.

Restore the call as
    .border_style(self.theme().error.clone())
matching the same pattern already used by the structured
"validation errors with suggestions" panel renderer in this file.
The border tone clone uses `self.theme()` (the new accessor), not
the old `self.theme` field that no longer exists.

Pure cosmetic restoration — no change to control flow or fallback
behavior in OutputMode::Plain / Quiet.

Cargo.lock left modified intentionally — same local-sibling-path
patch override situation flagged in the previous beads_rust
passes.
